chief_complaint: An 11-year-old female child presented with episodes of high-grade fever, diaphoresis, tachycardia, and hypertension.
history: The patient was diagnosed with tubercular meningitis (TBM) and underwent ventriculoperitoneal shunt surgery. She was stable post-operatively but developed symptoms of paroxysmal sympathetic hyperactivity (PSH) 3 days later.
exam: Vital signs: high-grade fever, tachycardia (100%), tachypnea, dystonia, hyperthermia, hypertension, and diaphoresis. Physical examination findings: consistent with PSH.
correct_diagnosis: Paroxysmal sympathetic hyperactivity (PSH) secondary to tubercular meningitis (TBM)
differentials: Paroxysmal sympathetic hyperactivity (PSH) secondary to tubercular meningitis (TBM)
correct_ranking: Paroxysmal sympathetic hyperactivity (PSH) secondary to tubercular meningitis (TBM)
